subject: Social Sciences
title: SA Provinces Explorer
tagline: Explore all 9 provinces — capitals, facts and famous landmarks!

pins.name: Gauteng
facts: Gauteng is the smallest province but the most populous.
Gauteng's name means 'place of gold' in Sotho.

pins.name: Western Cape
facts: Table Mountain is one of the New 7 Wonders of Nature.
The Cape Winelands are famous worldwide.

pins.name: KwaZulu-Natal
facts: The Drakensberg mountains run through KwaZulu-Natal.
Durban is South Africa’s busiest port city.

pins.name: Eastern Cape
facts: Addo Elephant National Park is in the Eastern Cape.
Nelson Mandela was born in the Eastern Cape.

pins.name: Limpopo
facts: Limpopo borders Zimbabwe, Botswana and Mozambique.
Part of the Kruger National Park lies in Limpopo.

pins.name: Mpumalanga
facts: Mpumalanga's name means 'place where the sun rises'.
The Blyde River Canyon is one of the largest canyons on Earth.

pins.name: North West
facts: The Sun City resort is in North West province.
North West is known for its maize and platinum mining.

pins.name: Free State
facts: Bloemfontein is known as the judicial capital of South Africa.
The Free State is known for its wide-open maize and sunflower fields.

pins.name: Northern Cape
facts: The Northern Cape is the largest but least populated province.
Kimberley is famous for its diamond mining history.
